zh.Barry Roe, Cork, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 科克巴利洛

zh.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry是Sir William Barry的兒子，於大約1395年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克巴特凡特。他於大約1420年與Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond在愛爾蘭庫迪 克萊爾布拉迪 卡素結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1444年在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克巴利洛享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry是Sir William Barry的儿子，于大约1395年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克巴特凡特。他于大约1420年与Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond在爱尔兰庫迪 克萊爾布拉迪 卡素结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1444年在爱尔兰庫迪 科克巴利洛享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Barr
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow) and northern Irish (Ulster): habitational name from any of various places in southwestern Scotland, in particular Ayrshire and Renfrewshire, named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’ or a Brittonic cognate of this.

English and Welsh: habitational name from Great Barr in Staffordshire. This is from Brittonic barro- ‘top, summit’, referring to Barr Beacon; there is evidence that this was known as la Bare in the 13th century.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Barre-en-Ouche in Eure, France, or perhaps from Barre-de-Semilly in Manche, France.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Scottish (Angus): habitational name from Barry in Angus or from another place so named, generally named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’, or from a British cognate of this word. This name is also the Scottish form of Barry .
French (Barrié): variant of Barrier .
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

variants.name: Berry
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Dubarry
meanings: French: variant of the topographic or habitational name Barry , with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’.

variants.name: Baril
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Alain, Armand, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean-Marc, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Monique, Phillippe.
French: from Old French baril ‘barrel’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cooper, or a nickname for a rotund man. Compare Barry 7 and Berry 7.

History: Jean Baril from the Saintonge region of France was in QC by 1673, when he married Marie Guillet, the first of his three wives. He died in 1724 in Batiscan, QC. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Barrie, Bar(r)y, and Berry.

variants.name: Bary
meanings: Walloon and northern French: variant of   Barry   8.
French (southern): rare variant of   Barry   6.
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

nameMeanings.name: Lawrence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized spelling of   Laurence .  This is the usual spelling of the surname, and is now becoming increasingly common as a given name, especially in North America.

nameMeanings.name: The
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the Irish name Barra . Since the 20th century this name has become very popular in the English-speaking world, especially Australia.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Barry .

nameMeanings.name: Roe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Joseph .  It has also been in favour as an independent given name since the 1990s.

variants.name: Joseph
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

en.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry was born about 1395, in Buttevant, County Cork, Ireland as the son of Sir William Barry. He married Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond about 1420, in Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1444, in Barryroe, County Cork, Ireland, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry est né environ 1395, à Buttevant, County Cork, Irlande, fils de Sir William Barry. Il a épousé Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond environ 1420, à Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1444, à Barryroe, County Cork, Irlande, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry wurde um 1395 in Buttevant, County Cork, Irland, als Sohn von Sir William Barry geboren. Er hat um 1420 in Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irland Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1444 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Barryroe, County Cork, Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry è nato il circa 1395 nel Buttevant, County Cork, Irlanda come figlio di Sir William Barry. Lui sposato Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond circa 1420, nel Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1444, nel Barryroe, County Cork, Irlanda, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.Barry Roe, Cork, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ コルクバーリロー
ja.Buttevant, Cork, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ コルクブッテヴァント
ja.Bunratty Castle, Connaught, Kingdom of T: アイルランドコウントイ クラーブンラッティ カストル
ja.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barryは約1395年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクブッテヴァントでSir William Barryの息子として生まれた。約1420年にアイルランドコウントイ クラーブンラッティ カストルでPrincess Orlagg O'Brien Thomondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1444年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクバーリローで50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry(은)는 대략 1395년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 버테베아트에서 Sir William Barry의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1420년 아일랜드 카운티 클래르 버래티 카스틀에서 Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1444년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 배리로에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry nasceu aproximadamente 1395, em Buttevant, County Cork, Irlanda filho de Sir William Barry. Ele casou-se com Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond aproximadamente 1420, em Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1444, em Barryroe, County Cork, Irlanda, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry родился около 1395 г. в местности буттевант, коунти корк, Ирландия. Его отца звали Sir William Barry. Он женился на женщине по имени Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond около 1420 г. в местности бунратти кастл, коунти клар, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1444 г. в местности барриро, коунти корк, Ирландия в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lawrence The Barry Roe Barry nació aproximadamente 1395, en Buttevant, County Cork, Irlanda como hijo de Sir William Barry. Se casó con Princess Orlagg O'Brien Thomond aproximadamente 1420, en Bunratty Castle, County Clare, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1444, en Barryroe, County Cork, Irlanda, a la edad de 50 años.
